title: Accounts Payable & Payroll
subhead: Managing cash out: Recording vendor bills and understanding payroll liabilities.

chunks.title: The Accounts Payable Cycle
chunks.bodyHtml:
When Skarion receives a bill from a vendor, we don't always pay it immediately. Instead, we enter it into our system as an Accounts Payable (AP).

This is a 2-step process under accrual accounting:

  1. Record the Bill: Debit Expense, Credit AP
  2. Pay the Bill: Debit AP, Credit Cash

hotspots.label: Invoice Number
hotspots.info: Crucial! Always enter the exact invoice number to avoid duplicate payments.

hotspots.label: Vendor Name
hotspots.info: Verify this matches our approved vendor list.

hotspots.label: Total Amount Due
hotspots.info: This is the amount we will credit to Accounts Payable.

chunks.title: Payroll: It's More Than Just Wages
chunks.bodyHtml:
When processing payroll, the gross wage isn't what the employee receives. You must withhold taxes and record employer taxes.

For a $5,000 gross payroll, the entry looks like this:

  • Debit Wages Expense: $5,000
  • Credit Payroll Liabilities (Taxes withheld): $1,000
  • Credit Cash (Net Pay): $4,000

chunks.title: Record a Vendor Bill
chunks.scenarioHtml: Skarion received a $500 bill from OfficePro Supplies. We will pay it in 30 days. Record the receipt of the bill.
